summary: AI workflow and decisioning platform for insurers and wealth firms.
editor_note: Financial workflow AI gets more interesting when every submission feeds the next decision rather than ending as a form. Feathery processes tens of millions of monthly submissions and serves 300+ insurance and wealth firms across onboarding, documents, and policy workflows. Unlike form builders, the bet is decisioning on top of operational data. The risk is proving cross-client learning without becoming a services project.

product_evidence.claim: The official product menu lists forms, document templates, document intelligence, meeting and audio intelligence, workflows, and a white-label editor.
The document-intelligence page claims structured extraction from documents with 98%+ accuracy for financial-services workflows.
The funding post says customers use Feathery for RIA onboarding, proposal generation, insurance submission intake, FNOL, policy checks, and benefit guides.

market_context.primary_user: Operations, underwriting, onboarding, and transformation teams at insurance and wealth-management firms.
market_context.differentiation: Feathery connects data collection, document processing, workflow routing, and decisioning in regulated financial workflows rather than staying at the form layer.
market_context.why_now: Insurance and wealth firms are trying to automate submission intake, onboarding, account opening, and policy checks without replacing every system of record.

risks.claim: The open question is whether Feathery can compound data across many client workflows while keeping regulated customers comfortable with model behavior and data boundaries.
risks.basis: VentureDex assessment from the official product pages, docs link, and Series A announcement.
